title: Central Theme binding Rebuild Of Eva
display_title: Central Theme binding Rebuild Of Eva
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS] Central Theme binding Rebuild Of Eva             Quote Post by ARS.SWK » Tue Apr 30, 2013 5:00 pm After watching 3.33 (UTW's subbed version) I've come to the conclusion that Rebuild's "story" or "theme" pertains to the question of whether or not to pilot Eva. Think about it: the majority of the plot centers on Shinji debating whether or not he should pilot Eva. Similarly, it is the central question that relates to Anno and whether or not he too should "pilot" Eva. To take this a little deeper, Anno has always said how Shinji essentially represents himself and how he views the surrounding environment. With Rebuild, he is questioning if he should "rebuild" or "pilot" Eva. Seems like a trivially simple question, but think of the implications of piloting Eva. For Anno, this means revisiting the time in his life where he underwent his nervous breakdown and existential angst. As all of us can recall, episodes 25/26 were the introspective and extractive viewpoints of his angst. Most likely, Rebuild is the question of whether or not to confront these feelings he had when he first created Eva. I feel that this means the same thing for Shinji and how he too will have to "revisit" the times in his life which caused him to have the view of the world that he has. Sequel theory be damned, this could mean confronting his issues with Gendo and Yui's absence from his formative years and how Shinji ponders the question of if he is alone, if he can advance, if he can redo, and whatever the fourth title may be. If Sequel Theory is confirmed, the implications are much, much heavier. What are your guys' thoughts on this theme? Is this what Anno is going for?

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Tue Apr 30, 2013 7:00 pm I like it, I like it! If we're thinking that way, then what does this theory say about the characterization of Asuka and Rei? Or Mari being there to "destroy" Eva somehow?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Apr 30, 2013 7:03 pm C.A.P. wrote: I like it, I like it! If we're thinking that way, then what does this theory say about the characterization of Asuka and Rei? Or Mari being there to "destroy" Eva somehow?

[]: [SPOILERS] Central Theme binding Rebuild Of Eva             Quote Post by ARS.SWK » Tue Apr 30, 2013 5:00 pm After watching 3.33 (UTW's subbed version) I've come to the conclusion that Rebuild's "story" or "theme" pertains to the question of whether or not to pilot Eva. Think about it: the majority of the plot centers on Shinji debating whether or not he should pilot Eva. Similarly, it is the central question that relates to Anno and whether or not he too should "pilot" Eva. To take this a little deeper, Anno has always said how Shinji essentially represents himself and how he views the surrounding environment. With Rebuild, he is questioning if he should "rebuild" or "pilot" Eva. Seems like a trivially simple question, but think of the implications of piloting Eva. For Anno, this means revisiting the time in his life where he underwent his nervous breakdown and existential angst. As all of us can recall, episodes 25/26 were the introspective and extractive viewpoints of his angst. If Sequel Theory is confirmed, the implications are much, much heavier. What are your guys' thoughts on this theme? Is this what Anno is going for?
